footer.tagline: The Agrobot Competition challenges students to build innovative robots for the agriculture of the future.

hero.subtitle: Build the agricultural robot of the future. Learn, innovate and win — together with your team.
hero.challengeText: Design, build and program an autonomous robot that performs real agricultural tasks. Together with your team you compete for the title — and gain experience you won't find anywhere else.

home.whySubtitle: The Agrobot Competition offers a unique chance to develop your technical and personal skills.

features.title: Teamwork
features.description: Collaborate with students from different programmes and develop your teamwork skills.

features.title: Technology
features.description: Learn to work with robotics, sensors, AI and other cutting-edge technologies.

features.title: Competition
features.description: Compete for the grand prize and show what your team can achieve.

features.title: Experience
features.description: Gain hands-on experience that strengthens your CV and prepares you for the future.

home.videoSubtitle: Discover what previous participants built and experienced.
home.ctaText: Sign up today and become part of the Agrobot Competition. The future of agriculture starts with you!

rules.heroSubtitle: Everything you need to know about the setup, the programme and the rules of the Agrobot Competition.

timeline.title: Registration
timeline.desc: Register your team and choose your minor.

timeline.title: Kickoff
timeline.desc: Introduction, team formation and first workshops.

timeline.title: Build phase
timeline.desc: Design, build and test your agrobot.

timeline.title: Competition day
timeline.desc: Show off your robot and compete for the grand prize!

rulesList: Each team consists of at least 3 and at most 6 students.
The robot must be able to navigate a test field autonomously.
Use of standard safety equipment is mandatory.
All team members must be enrolled in an accredited programme.
The robot may not weigh more than 50 kg.
Teams receive a budget of €500 for materials.
rules.downloadsText: Download the full regulations and the programme booklet.

faq.heroSubtitle: Find answers to the most common questions about the Agrobot Competition.

items.a: The Agrobot Competition is an annual student competition in which teams build an autonomous robot that performs a task in the agricultural sector — think weed control, crop monitoring or harvesting.

items.q: What is the goal of the competition?
items.a: The goal is to inspire students to come up with innovative technological solutions for the agriculture of the future.
Robotics, artificial intelligence, precision agriculture, sustainability and collaboration are central.
All students at Dutch universities of applied sciences and research universities can participate, provided they are enrolled in an accredited programme.

items.q: How big should a team be?
items.a: A team consists of at least 3 and at most 6 students.
Participation is free. Teams receive a budget of €500 for materials.

items.q: When does the competition take place?
items.a: The competition day takes place in April. Registration opens in September and closes in mid-October.
Basic knowledge is handy but not required. You'll learn the necessary skills during workshops.

items.q: Do I have to be in a technical programme?
items.a: No! Teams benefit from diverse backgrounds — think marketing, design, project management and engineering.
Sign up for a relevant minor and join the kickoff workshops. There you'll learn everything you need.

faq.inspirationText: Watch videos and images from previous editions.

benefits.title: Hands-on experience
benefits.desc: Work on a real project with robots, sensors and AI.

benefits.title: Study credits
benefits.desc: Taking part through a minor earns you ECTS credits for your studies.

benefits.title: Network
benefits.desc: Meet experts, companies and fellow students from across the country.

testimonials.quote: The best experience of my studies. I learned so much about teamwork and technology.
testimonials.author: Lisa, HAS University

testimonials.quote: Our robot didn't work perfectly, but the process was priceless. Recommended for everyone!
testimonials.author: Sander, Avans University
